mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals rising capacity utilization and corporate pricing power, which expands profit margins and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly growth vs forward 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it indicates labor market slack that reduces aggregate demand and wage growth, compressing corporate profits and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% and is rising.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds trailing 12-month CPI inflation, real interest rates become positive and restrictive, tightening financial conditions and reducing equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the real rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY) vs forward 20-day SPX returns is below 0.05 in absolute value over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession recovery regime where GDP is accelerating from a trough, strong demand growth translates into rising corporate earnings and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery (GDPC1 sequential increase after two quarters of decline)
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.96) in the 12-month period following each NBER recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million annualized units signals a construction downturn that reduces durable goods demand, employment, and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 40% of months with HOUST < 1200.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls decline month-over-month and the unemployment rate simultaneously rises by more than 0.2 percentage points, the joint signal indicates a severe labor market contraction that sharply reduces consumption and equity valuations within the following week.
failure_condition: The average forward 5-day SPX return during joint trigger months is not negative, or the signal generates more than 20% false positives over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail and food services sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS month-over-month growth and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is negative or below 0.05 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflation regime where CPI inflation is falling from above 3% towards the target, falling inflation reduces discount rate uncertainty and improves expected real earnings, producing positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: disinflation regime (CPIAUCSL YoY declining from >3% and remaining above 2%)
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns are not positive on average during periods identified as disinflationary (CPIAUCSL YoY decline > 0.5% from a peak above 3%), with average return less than 1%.
